refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, enabling measurable verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Monitor and log link uptime/downtime over the evaluation period; calculate availability percentage and compare against target.
sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor link quality and report status to operators.

candidate_dps: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and adaptive bandwidth management.
DP3: Error detection and automatic reconnection protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability concerns and supports modular failover without tight coupling to other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple network paths or technologies.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication links and related network functions.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Status and health data of the network link sent to other system modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad system scope but less focused containment of link availability logic.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over network link redundancy.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation link management.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not manage link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Coordination needed with Safety Services for alerting on link failures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Safety Services for failure alerts and recovery coordination.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network link architecture) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and supports measurable availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if link monitoring logic is duplicated outside Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment of DP responsibilities, minimizing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link logs is feasible within the Teleoperation module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Enhance coordination interfaces with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
Plan for integration of monitoring and recovery DPs in next refinement.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, integrating additional DPs for monitoring and recovery will improve robustness and safety alignment.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Monitor link quality and report status to operators.

revise.why: To incorporate complementary design parameters addressing link health monitoring and automatic recovery mechanisms.

assumptions: Multiple network paths are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module can interface effectively with Safety Services.
open_risks: Complexity of redundant network architecture may impact maintainability.
Potential single point of failure in Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Network availability performance data from prototype or simulation.
Interface definitions and test plans for Teleoperation-Safety Services coordination.
